matches.moment: A Kessié free kick forces Room into a parried save; Diallo attacks the rebound before the defensive recovery is complete.

Ecuador push forward desperately after HT; Reijnders drives into the vacated central space; Undav is fouled in the box — penalty converted; Beier's late counter seals it as ECU are exposed at the back.
Sweden equalize through Isak on a quick transition, briefly threatening upset; Kamada punishes Sweden's high defensive line on a precisely-timed run in the 68th minute.
Netherlands learn Japan are winning in parallel at halftime; Koeman pushes for a 4th — Dumfries' overlapping run creates the third, neutralizing Japan's GD calculation.

USA score early on a Freeman delivery set piece; Güler — the tournament's most creative player with 0 goals to show for it — finally converts a curling shot from 20m after Çalhanoğlu's diagonal releases him.

A Paraguay corner from the right — Romero's flick creates chaos in the six-yard box; Gómez pounces on the second ball after Souttar's clearance falls short; Australia's shape collapses for the only time in the match.

verdict: Today is the day the World Cup gets real. Six simultaneous final group-stage matches across three groups produce a day defined not by star power but by structural incentives and their consequences. The story of Groups D, E, and F entering today is one of remarkable compression: in Group D, two teams at three points each fight for one knockout spot while a technically gifted Türkiye side — with the creative trio of Güler, Çalhanoğlu, and Yıldız — leaves the tournament having created more chances than their zero goals suggest and finding their most deserved return only when it no longer matters. In Group E, the Ecuador experiment collapses under the weight of zero tournament goals and the absence of Kendry Páez, the one player who could have troubled a rotating Germany side; Germany advance with the kind of unremarkable 2-0 that conceals an enormous talent reserve, and Ivory Coast grind out the result they need against a Curaçao side whose goalkeeper Eloy Room produced the most extraordinary individual defensive performance of this tournament so far. Group F resolves cleanly: the Netherlands' goal-difference ambitions drive them to a wide margin against an already-eliminated Tunisia; Japan's counter-attacking precision produces exactly the efficient win their system is designed to deliver; and Sweden — carrying Gyökeres and Isak but lacking the tactical discipline to survive a knockout-style pressure match — exit with a 2-1 defeat that reflects their honest place in the tournament hierarchy. The Day 11 slate confirms what the xG data has been saying since matchday 1: at this World Cup, systems beat individuals in group play. Ecuador had Caicedo. Turkey had Güler. Both go home. Germany and Japan, teams that suppressed their individual talent within functional structures, advance with clean profiles and energy preserved for the rounds that matter. — The Analyst.

players.name: Ayase Ueda
players.watch: Exploit Sweden's high defensive line on Japan's third-man runs; aerial threat on set pieces

players.name: Arda Güler
players.stat: 0 tournament goals, est. 1.3 xG/game created — most creative on an eliminated side
players.watch: The tournament goal that was coming — 20-25m curling finish after Çalhanoğlu build-up

players.name: Cody Gakpo
players.watch: First goal of the match to settle Netherlands and start the GD accumulation process

players.name: Deniz Undav
players.stat: 5 sub goal contributions this tournament (ties Roger Milla's all-time WC bench record)
players.watch: Starting today vs. Ecuador — first full 90 minutes; intelligent movement in and around Ecuador's 3-5-2

players.name: Viktor Gyökeres
players.stat: 7 goals in qualifying campaign (fallback — no WC tournament goals yet)
players.watch: Must-win final group game; Sweden's tournament ends without him delivering against Japan

players.name: Diego Gómez
players.stat: 0 tournament goals; stepping up with Almirón suspended
players.watch: The decisive second-ball moment from a set piece — the one Paraguay goal that keeps their World Cup alive
